design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, execute plays, score goals, manage line changes and strategy across exhibition or playoff matches
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time puck control and positioning versus opponent AI; managing player fatigue and injuries
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic NHL team management and competitive hockey play with real player rosters and team tactics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes realism and depth over arcade accessibility—includes injury system, shattering glass details, and strategic lineup management; EA Sports' commitment to authenticity despite lacking NHL branding (NHLPA-only license)

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty can be adjusted through AI opponent selection; AI described as not sufficiently challenging—'scoring goals is still too easy' indicates accessibility-first design rather than punishing difficulty

progression.unlock_mechanism: Game modes include single-game exhibition and playoff progression (single-elimination or best-of-7 format); trophy awarded for playoff completion
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer competitive mode with second player
Multiple team rosters from 1991-92 NHL season (24 teams with customizable lines)
Playoff bracket progression with championship trophy
Adjustable AI difficulty for varied challenge levels

world_building_techniques: Authentic NHL team cities (though generic naming without team nicknames due to licensing)
Real player names from 1991-92 rosters create authenticity without traditional narrative
Stanley Cup-style trophy as victory reward reinforces sports prestige context

faction_indicators: 24 distinct NHL teams with regional identities (Long Island vs. New York differentiation)
Team rosters reflect actual 1991-92 player affiliations
danger_signals: Injury system creates consequences for aggressive play
Opposing team AI provides dynamic threat modeling
environmental_storytelling: Ice rink arena environments with detail (shattering glass feedback)
Stadium presentations differentiate team home-ice advantage
Playoff progression toward Stanley Cup symbolizes competitive hierarchy
